shortDescription: SPEC-242C Quality Polish + SPEC-242D Monte Carlo Simulation & Equity Waterfall Distribution
problem: SPEC-242C had 5 quality issues (scattered formatters, unsafe casts, no error resilience, complex type inference). SPEC-242D: No probabilistic analysis or LP/GP profit distribution tools existed in the Interest Cost Calculator.
cause: SPEC-242C was functional but below Google-level quality standard. SPEC-242D was the next phase in the ADR-242 roadmap — advanced financial modeling features.
solution: TASK A: Centralized formatCurrencyWhole() across 4 components, replaced `as Record` with getObject(), added try-catch per project in aggregator, exported DebtMaturityFormData type. TASK B: Monte Carlo engine (Mulberry32 PRNG, 3 distributions, histogram via Sturges' rule, fan chart), Waterfall engine (tiered LP/GP, 3 presets, Newton IRR), MonteCarloTab (fan chart + histogram + stats), EquityWaterfallDialog (stacked bar + summary), integrated as 9th tab + header button in InterestCostDialog.
testing: TypeScript compilation — 0 new errors on changed files. Vercel auto-deploy triggered.
notes: MC engine: seeded RNG for reproducibility, reuses npv-engine + sensitivity-engine. Waterfall: supports LP-first and pari-passu. ~1,594 new lines across 4 new + 11 modified files.
relatedBackups: SPEC-242A Sensitivity Analysis & DSCR Stress Testing
SPEC-242C Portfolio Dashboard & Debt Maturity Wall

commits.message: fix: SPEC-242C quality — centralize formatters, type safety, error resilience
feat: SPEC-242D Monte Carlo Simulation & Equity Waterfall Distribution
